ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Na ɔsoɛɛ hɔ anafua no; na oyii dza ɔwɔ ne nkyɛn no mu bi dɛ ɔdze rekɛkyɛ no nua Esau;
translated: And he was there at the place; and he took that which was near him, that he would give to his brother Esau.

ref: Let's sell him to the Ishmaelites and not harm him. After all, he is our brother.” And the others agreed.
text: Hom mbra ma yɛntɔn no mma Ismaelfo yi, na mma wɔmma yɛmmfa hɛn nsa nnka no; osiandɛ hɛn nua a, hɛn bɔgyaa a. Na no nuanom no tsiee no.
translated: Let us not sell this to these Israelites, and do not let them lay their hands on it; if it concerns their brother, their enemy. And his brothers listened to it.

ref: she called in her servants and said, “Look! This Hebrew has come just to make fools of us. He tried to rape me, but I screamed for help.
text: ɔfrɛfrɛɛ no nkorɔfo a wɔwɔ ne fie no, na ɔkaa kyerɛɛ hɔn dɛ, Wɔnhwɛ, ɔakɛfa Hebriwnyi aba ma oribesi hɛn atwetwe; ɔbaa me nkyɛn dɛ ɔnye me rebɛda, na medze ndze kɛse kekaa mu:
translated: He/she called the people who were in his/her house and told them, “Do not look, he will bring the Hebrews to their gathering; he came to me saying he will lie with me, and I will place a great stone there.”

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Na esee me dɛ, Ampaara mebɛyɛ wo yie, na mama w’asefo ayɛ dɛ mpoano anhwea a no dodow ntsi wɔkan a ɔnnyɛ.
translated: It pleased me, truly I will do you well, and I will make your followers happy, the sea’s shore that many say does not exist.

text: Na Leah n’abaawa Zilpa woo babanyin maa Jakob.
translated: Leah and Zilpah, the mothers of Jacob.
